intro: Caucaea chimborazoensis is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Ecuador, characterized by its unique, funnel-shaped flowers. Traditionally, Caucaea chimborazoensis is used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Caucaea chimborazoensis is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
